ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "street" TEXT /* Street address of the customer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the customer */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the customer */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the customer record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the customer record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each preference entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer associated with this preference */,
  "content_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the content type preferred by the customer */,
  "interval_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the interval at which the customer prefers to receive communications */,
  "preferred_channel" TEXT /* Preferred communication channel of the customer (e.g., email, SMS, social media) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_preferences_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_preferences_content_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("content_type_id") REFERENCES content_types ("content_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_preferences_interval_id FOREIGN KEY ("interval_id") REFERENCES intervals ("interval_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "communication_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each communication log entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who received the communication */,
  "communication_type" TEXT /* Type of communication sent (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  "sent_date" TEXT /* Date when the communication was sent */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the communication */,
  "click_through_rate" REAL /* Click-through rate of the communication */,
  "open_rate" REAL /* Open rate of the communication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communication_logs_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the marketing campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the campaign (e.g., active, completed) */,
  "budget" TEXT /* Total budget allocated for the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_participants" (
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each participant entry */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the campaign the customer participated in */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer participating in the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("participant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_participants_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_participants_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_types" (
  "content_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the content type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the content type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("content_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "intervals" (
  "interval_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interval */,
  "interval_name" TEXT /* Name of the interval (e.g., Daily, Weekly) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the interval */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interval_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., Admin, Marketer) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Role assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer providing feedback */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the campaign related to the feedback */,
  "feedback_text" TEXT /* Text of the feedback provided by the customer */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating provided by the customer (e.g., 1 to 5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "preferences_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer whose preference was changed */,
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* ID of the preference that was changed */,
  "changed_on" TEXT /* Date when the preference was changed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_preferences_history_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_preferences_history_preference_id FOREIGN KEY ("preference_id") REFERENCES preferences ("preference_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segments" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer in the segment */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the segment the customer belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_segments_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_segments_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES segments ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "channels" (
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each channel */,
  "channel_name" TEXT /* Name of the channel (e.g., email, SMS, social media) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the channel */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_channel" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping entry */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the campaign */,
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the channel used by the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_channel_channel_id FOREIGN KEY ("channel_id") REFERENCES channels ("channel_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_channel_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the campaign */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., open rate, click-through rate) */,
  "metric_value" TEXT /* Value of the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_metrics_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id")
)
